children.heading: .—Indirect Cost That Violates a FAR Cost Principle
children.label: a .—Indirect Cost That Violates a FAR Cost Principle
children.text: The head of an agency shall require that a covered contract provide that if the contractor submits to the agency a proposal for settlement of indirect costs incurred by the contractor for any period after such costs have been accrued and if that proposal includes the submission of a cost which is unallowable because the cost violates a cost principle in the Federal Acquisition Regulation or applicable agency supplement to the Federal Acquisition Regulation, the cost shall be disallowed.

children.heading: Penalty for Violation of Cost Principle.—
children.label: b Penalty for Violation of Cost Principle.—
A
children.text: the amount of the disallowed cost allocated to covered contracts for which a proposal for settlement of indirect costs has been submitted; plus

children.label: B
children.text: interest (to be computed based on provisions in the Federal Acquisition Regulation) to compensate the United States for the use of any funds which a contractor has been paid in excess of the amount to which the contractor was entitled.

children.heading: .—Cost determined to be unallowable before proposal submitted
children.label: 2 .—Cost determined to be unallowable before proposal submitted
children.text: If the head of the agency determines that a proposal for settlement of indirect costs submitted by a contractor includes a cost determined to be unallowable in the case of such contractor before the submission of such proposal, the head of the agency shall assess a penalty against the contractor in an amount equal to two times the amount of the disallowed cost allocated to covered contracts for which a proposal for settlement of indirect costs has been submitted.
the contractor withdraws the proposal before the formal initiation of an audit of the proposal by the Federal Government and resubmits a revised proposal;
the amount of unallowable costs subject to the penalty is insignificant; or

children.label: A
children.text: it has established appropriate policies and personnel training and an internal control and review system that provide assurances that unallowable costs subject to penalties are precluded from being included in the contractor’s proposal for settlement of indirect costs; and

children.label: B
children.text: the unallowable costs subject to the penalty were inadvertently incorporated into the proposal.

children.heading: .—Applicability of Contract Disputes Procedure to Disallowance of Cost and Assessment of Penalty
children.label: d .—Applicability of Contract Disputes Procedure to Disallowance of Cost and Assessment of Penalty

children.text: shall be considered a final decision for the purposes of ; andsection 7103 of title 41
is appealable in the manner provided in .section 7104(a) of title 41
